---
title: "CBI Presentation"
author: "Olivier Amacker"
date: 2026-07-24
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, ../assets/style/presentation.scss]
    center: true
    transition: fade
    incremental: false
    slide-number: true
    preview-links: auto
    navigation-mode: grid
    # auto-play-media: true
    footer: '<a href="../index.qmd" style="color: inherit; text-decoration: none;">Home</a> | <a href="../sources.qmd" style="color: inherit; text-decoration: none;">Sources</a> | CBI Presentation © 2026'
bibliography: ../../bachelor-thesis-report/bibliography.bib
---

# What Is The Goal Of This Presentation?

- Explain what I have done so far
- Showcase my main results
- Explain what the next steps could be

# What Have I Done So Far?

 - Reproduced the @Alizadeh2025.12.22.695524 paper regarding {{< acr NPVR >}} of {{<acr MRI >}} graph metrics
 - Perturbed fMRIPrep (@fMRIPrep) runs via Verificarlo (@denis2018verificarlocheckingfloatingpoint) and assessed the {{< acr FC >}} matrices changes
 - Perturbed {{< acr FC >}} matrices calculation and assessed the {{< acr PCA >}} features extraction changes

# @Alizadeh2025.12.22.695524 Paper Goals

The paper has 2 goals:

 - Demonstrate on simulated data that {{< acr NPVR >}} increase uncertainty in Cohen's d estimates, with stronger effects at small n.
 - Assess how {{< acr MCA >}} perturbations via Verificarlo of fMRIPrep runs impact the {{< acr NPVR >}} of various graph metrics.

Here is the formula for the {{< acr NPVR >}}:

$$NPVR = \frac{\sigma_{num}}{\sigma_{pop}}$$

Where $\sigma_{num}$ is the numerical variability and $\sigma_{pop}$ is the population variability.


# @Alizadeh2025.12.22.695524 Paper Reproduction Code

The code used for this section is available online: [https://olivier.amacker.dev/bachelor-thesis/site/notebooks/npvr-simulation/](https://olivier.amacker.dev/bachelor-thesis/site/notebooks/npvr-simulation/){target="_blank"}

![NPVR notebook {{< acr QR >}} code](../assets/presentations/cbi-presentation/npvr-simulation/qr-code.svg){#fig-npvr-qr}

# @Alizadeh2025.12.22.695524 Paper NPVR Results Comparison

::: {layout="[ [1, 1, 1], [1, 1, 1] ]"}

![Population distribution (mine)](../assets/presentations/cbi-presentation/npvr-simulation/populations_plot.png){#fig-npvr-populations width="100%" height="150px"}

![Relation between the NPVR and the Cohen's $d$ (mine)](../assets/presentations/cbi-presentation/npvr-simulation/npvr_sample_size_cohens_delta_variation.png){#fig-npvr-sample-size width="100%" height="150px"}

![Relation between pop and num var (mine)](../assets/presentations/cbi-presentation/npvr-simulation/npvr_dpop_dnum_variation.png){#fig-dnum-variation width="100%" height="150px"}

![Population distribution (paper)](../assets/presentations/cbi-presentation/npvr-simulation-original/population.png){#fig-original-population width="100%" height="150px"}

![Relation between the NPVR and the Cohen's $d$ (paper)](../assets/presentations/cbi-presentation/npvr-simulation-original/cohens.png){#fig-original-cohens width="100%" height="150px"}

![Relation between pop and num var (paper)](../assets/presentations/cbi-presentation/npvr-simulation-original/NPVR.png){#fig-original-npvr width="100%" height="150px"}

:::

# @Alizadeh2025.12.22.695524 Paper {{< acr PR >}}

Regarding the Relation between the NPVR and the Cohen's $d$ differences, a {{< acr PR >}} was open and merged into the paper's repository (see [here](https://github.com/mina94az/Numerical-Variability-of-functional-MRI-Graph-Measures/pull/3){target="_blank"})

![Hard coded values for the 2nd graph](../assets/presentations/cbi-presentation/npvr-simulation/npvr_pr.png){#fig-npvr-pr height="100px"}

# @Alizadeh2025.12.22.695524 Paper Graph Metrics

Here are the different graph metrics that were computed once with and once without confounds regression

4 local graph metrics were computed:

  - Degree centrality
  - Clustering coefficient
  - Betweenness centrality
  - Eigenvector centrality

2 global metrics:

  - Small-worldness
  - Average shortest path length

  
# @Alizadeh2025.12.22.695524 Paper Reproduction Code

The code used for this section is available online: [https://olivier.amacker.dev/bachelor-thesis/site/notebooks/fuzzy-fmriprep-graph-metrics-analysis.html](https://olivier.amacker.dev/bachelor-thesis/site/notebooks/fuzzy-fmriprep-graph-metrics-analysis.html){target="_blank"}

![Graph Metrics notebook {{< acr QR >}} code](../assets/presentations/cbi-presentation/graph-metrics/qr_code.svg){#fig-graph-metrics-qr}


# @Alizadeh2025.12.22.695524 Paper Graph Metrics Results Comparison

::: {layout="[1, 1]"}

![Graph metrics {{< acr NPVR >}} per threshold (mine)](../assets/presentations/cbi-presentation/graph-metrics/graph_metrics_npvr.png){#fig-graph-metrics-threshold width="100%" height="500px"}

![Graph metrics {{< acr NPVR >}} per threshold (paper)](../assets/presentations/cbi-presentation/graph-metrics-original/graph_metrics_npvr.png){#fig-original-graph-metrics-threshold width="100%" height="500px"}

:::

# @Alizadeh2025.12.22.695524 Paper Graph Metrics Results Comparison

::: {layout="[1, 1]"}

![Variation of the NPVR between without and with confound (mine)](../assets/presentations/cbi-presentation/graph-metrics/graph_metrics_npvr_confound_difference.png){#fig-npvr-populations width="100%" height="500px"}

![Variation of the NPVR between without and with confound (paper)](../assets/presentations/cbi-presentation/graph-metrics-original/graph_metrics_npvr_confound_difference.png){#fig-npvr-sample-size width="100%" height="500"}
:::


# @Alizadeh2025.12.22.695524 Paper Graph Metrics Results Comparison

::: {layout="[1, 1]"}

![Betweenness centrality {{< acr NPVR >}} values across brain regions and thresholds (mine)](../assets/presentations/cbi-presentation/graph-metrics/npvr_across_brain_regions_betweenness_centrality.png){#fig-npvr-populations width="100%" height="500px"}

![{{< acr NPVR >}} values across brain regions and thresholds (paper)](../assets/presentations/cbi-presentation/graph-metrics-original/npvr_across_brain_regions.png){#fig-npvr-sample-size width="100%" height="500"}
:::

# @Alizadeh2025.12.22.695524 Paper Graph Metrics Results Comparison

::: {layout="[1, 1]"}

![Clustering coefficient {{< acr NPVR >}} values across brain regions and thresholds (mine)](../assets/presentations/cbi-presentation/graph-metrics/npvr_across_brain_regions_clustering_coefficient.png){#fig-npvr-populations width="100%" height="500px"}

![{{< acr NPVR >}} values across brain regions and thresholds (paper)](../assets/presentations/cbi-presentation/graph-metrics-original/npvr_across_brain_regions.png){#fig-npvr-sample-size width="100%" height="500"}
:::

# @Alizadeh2025.12.22.695524 Paper Graph Metrics Results Comparison

::: {layout="[1, 1]"}

![Eigenvector centrality {{< acr NPVR >}} values across brain regions and thresholds (mine)](../assets/presentations/cbi-presentation/graph-metrics/npvr_across_brain_regions_eigenvector_centrality.png){#fig-npvr-populations width="100%" height="500px"}

![{{< acr NPVR >}} values across brain regions and thresholds (paper)](../assets/presentations/cbi-presentation/graph-metrics-original/npvr_across_brain_regions.png){#fig-npvr-sample-size width="100%" height="500"}
:::

# @Alizadeh2025.12.22.695524 Paper Graph Metrics Results Comparison

::: {layout="[1, 1]"}

![Degree centrality {{< acr NPVR >}} values across brain regions and thresholds (mine)](../assets/presentations/cbi-presentation/graph-metrics/npvr_across_brain_regions_degree_centrality.png){#fig-npvr-populations width="100%" height="500px"}

![{{< acr NPVR >}} values across brain regions and thresholds (paper)](../assets/presentations/cbi-presentation/graph-metrics-original/npvr_across_brain_regions.png){#fig-npvr-sample-size width="100%" height="500"}
:::

# Perturbated FMRIPrep Runs {{< acr FC >}} Matrices Analysis Goals

The goal of this section was to take the {{< acr MCA >}} perturbation via Verificarlo of fMRIPrep runs from the previous section, and examine the impact of the perturbations on the {{< acr FC >}} matrices

# Perturbed FMRIPrep Runs {{< acr FC >}} Matrices Analysis Code

The code used for this section is available online: [https://olivier.amacker.dev/bachelor-thesis/site/notebooks/fuzzy-fmriprep-fc-matrices-analysis.html](https://olivier.amacker.dev/bachelor-thesis/site/notebooks/fuzzy-fmriprep-fc-matrices-analysis.html){target="_blank"}

![Fuzzy {{< acr FC >}} matrices notebook {{< acr QR >}} code](../assets/presentations/cbi-presentation/fuzzy-fc-matrices/qr_code.svg){#fig-npvr-qr}

# Perturbated FMRIPrep Runs {{< acr FC >}} Matrices Analysis Results

::: {layout="[1, 1]"}

![{{< acr NPVR >}} across {{< acr FC >}} matrices edge](../assets/presentations/cbi-presentation/fuzzy-fc-matrices/npvr_heatmap.png){#fig-npvr-populations width="100%" height="500px"}

![{{< acr NPVR >}} across {{< acr FC >}} matrices edge difference bewteen with and without global confound regression](../assets/presentations/cbi-presentation/fuzzy-fc-matrices/npvr_delta_heatmap.png){#fig-npvr-sample-size width="100%" height="500"}
:::

# References

::: {#refs}
:::

# Annex
Here is how the NPVR is calculated in details:

$$
  \sigma_{num}^2 = \frac{1}{m} \sum_{j=1}^m \left[ \frac{1}{n-1} \sum_{i=1}^n \left( x_{i,j} - \bar x_{.,j}\right)^2\right]
$$

$$
  \sigma_{pop}^2 = \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^n \left[ \frac{1}{m-1} \sum_{j=1}^m \left( x_{i,j} - \bar x_{i,.}\right)^2\right]
$$

$$
  NPVR = \frac{\sigma_{num}}{\sigma_{pop}}
$$
